content:
===BEGIN===
Antibiotic resistance is becoming a serious global problem because the bacteria that cause infections are evolving to survive even our strongest medicines. This happens largely because antibiotics are often overused or misused—for example, when they are prescribed for viral infections like the common cold, where they have no effect. In agriculture, antibiotics are also used extensively to promote growth in healthy animals, which further encourages resistant bacteria to develop. As resistant bacteria multiply, infections become harder and more expensive to treat, leading to longer hospital stays and increased risk of death. Because bacteria can spread quickly from person to person and around the world, resistance is spreading rapidly. To slow this trend, it is crucial to use antibiotics only when truly necessary and to develop new treatments. For business leaders, understanding this issue is important as antibiotic resistance threatens public health, disrupts economies, and increases healthcare costs globally.
